item.title: Oil storage, refineries and transport all in the firing line of Iran-US-Israeli war
item.description: Drones and missiles have landed on key refineries and depots, destroying millions of barrels of oil production. ABC NEWS Verify maps key locations destroyed or targeted during the war.

item.title: Jonah Pezet wants more after helping Parramatta Eels to upset NRL win over Brisbane Broncos
item.description: Parramatta playmaker Jonah Pezet destroyed the team he will play for next year — but in a sign of his appetite for excellence, he was far from satisfied.

item.title: Australia releases six days' worth of petrol and five of diesel from emergency stockpile
item.description: Australia will make available about six days' worth of petrol from its emergency stockpile and five days of diesel, the first use of its fuel reserves since the invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

item.title: FIFA, AFC accused of failing their human rights obligations with Iranian women's footballers
item.description: Pressure is growing on FIFA and the Asian Football Confederation to help the members of the Iranian women's football team returning home, and their families, and to learn lessons ahead of the men's World Cup.

item.title: India continues to strengthen during ICC boss Jay Shah's tenure, but it may be coming at cricket's cost
item.description: India releases a video of its celebrations following the T20 World Cup, and it features an unhealthy amount of a man who should not be there.

item.title: Qantas COVID pandemic flight credits could be on their way to customers this year
item.description: Qantas agrees to settle a class action ​related to flight credits issued during the COVID-19 ‌pandemic.

item.title: Interruptions, campaign woes and 'razzle-dazzle': Here's how the SA leaders debate played out
item.description: As South Australians get ready to vote in a week's time, Premier Peter Malinauskas and Opposition Leader Ashton Hurn went head-to-head in a campaign debate. Here's what they said.

item.title: Byron Bay's famous Bluesfest cancelled with less than three weeks' notice
item.description: Thousands of music fans will be left scrambling to rearrange their Easter plans after one of Australia's last major festivals was called off with less than three weeks' notice.

item.title: Former Taiwan men's coach kicked out of Women's Asian Cup match for 'political chants'
item.description: The Women's Asian Cup has been embroiled in another diplomatic controversy, with Taiwanese officials lodging a complaint after a former national coach was ejected from a stadium for leading a chant which included the word "Taiwan."

item.title: Cape York and Port Hedland flagged as Australia's highest risk entry points for lumpy skin disease
item.description: Researchers have mapped the northern gateways where the cattle disease, lumpy skin disease, could first enter Australia, identifying Cape York Peninsula and Port Hedland as the highest-risk locations.

item.title: Parents in Alice Springs worry Birth and Beyond will close due to funding shortfall
item.description: An "incredible" organisation delivering pregnancy and postnatal support in Alice Springs is calling for funding from both Northern Territory and federal governments to keep their doors open.

item.title: Western Australia the big GST winner again, despite massive budget surpluses
item.description: WA will get an extra $6.6 billion from taxpayers in the coming financial year under its special GST deal, despite being the only state with a budget surplus.

item.title: How are rising fuel costs and oil supply shortages affecting you or your business?
item.description: As fuel costs soar and the global economy reacts to a shortage of oil supplies, we need your help in identifying the impacts here in Australia. Whether it's the cost of your work commute or supply shortages affecting your business, share it with us.

item.title: Flooding at Daly River community worst on record as Katherine mayor calls for Defence assistance
item.description: Flooding in the remote Northern Territory town of Daly River has become the community's worst on record, surpassing the previous highest water level recorded in 1998.

item.title: What to watch, read and listen to this weekend: A new quiz show and a lovable goat
item.description: This weekend try an unforgettable feta recipe or get lost in the hypnotic rhythms of legendary desert blues band Tinariwen's new album.

item.title: After almost 130 years in England, an Aboriginal man has been reburied on Country
item.description: After almost 130 years, the remains of an Aboriginal man have made the long journey home from Oxford University to be reburied on the Country from where he was taken at Berowra Creek in Sydney.

item.title: Winter Paralympics coverage still sorely lacking, athletes and supporters say
item.description: The Paralympic Winter Games are in full swing at Milano Cortina, and athletes and supporters have expressed their frustration over the lack of coverage of the event.

item.title: Iranian footballers backed by Afghan international cricketer Firooza Amiri to adjust to life in Australia
item.description: Having fled Afghanistan as an 18-year-old, international cricketer Firooza Amiri knows what it is like to be a sports refugee from a war-torn country.

item.title: Cuba to release 51 people from prison in unexpected move
item.description: Cuba's government has said it would release 51 people from the island's prisons in an unexpected move.

item.title: Katherine experienced major flooding in 1998
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Local member Jo Hersey emotional amid Katherine floods
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Katherine floods bring back memories of fatal '98 flooding, victims urged to seek mental health help
item.description: When Member of the Legislative Assembly Jo Hersey stood up for a media interview about the floods in her home town Katherine last weekend, she had tears in her eyes.

item.title: Fuel panic is spreading and now Australia is tapping into its emergency fuel supplies
item.description: The country's stockpile of fuel is about to be opened but experts are warning it won't solve the problem of soaring prices or shortage fears.

item.title: WA bikie law change leaves police powerless to stop convicted child sex offenders from associating
item.description: Changes in Western Australia's anti-consorting laws have had the unintended consequence of making it easier for convicted child sex offenders to associate with each other.

item.title: Hawthorn forward Dylan Moore allegedly found with bag of 'crystalline substance' after US arrest, police documents claim
item.description: Hawthorn forward Dylan Moore was allegedly found with a bag of a "crystalline substance" and a rolled up $100 dollar bill when he and teammate Connor Macdonald were arrested last year, Arizona police documents claim.

item.title: A bruising week for Labor after an email blunder, but the blowtorch is back on the Liberals after a candidate's comments
item.description: With two weeks before polls, Labor were in damage control after a health email blunder. It should have been a chance for the Liberals to get ahead, but after controversial comments by a candidate it's the one left bruised.

item.title: How young Australian actors are making a career in a tough industry
item.description: Young actors dream of careers on stage or screen but they know finding full-time work is a tough gig, despite plenty of famous Australians making it in Hollywood.

item.title: Australian hospitals on alert after Iranian hackers attack medical technology company Stryker
item.description: Hospitals across the nation are on alert after an Iranian cyber militia linked to the Islamic regime hacked a US multinational that supplies critical equipment to Australia.

item.title: Ruling rendering NSW parliament powerless to compel witnesses used for first time
item.description: James O'Brien from Honeywell, which is subcontracted to manage facilities at Newcastle's Calvary Mater Hospital, failed to turn up to an inquiry.

item.title: 'Get me out of here': Johnston says fans on the field was fun until it wasn't
item.description: Alex Johnston becomes the greatest try-scorer in Australian rugby league history with a double against the Roosters, prompting fans to ignore the NRL's pleas not to storm the field to celebrate.

item.title: Only service station in NSW town of Batlow runs out of fuel amid surging prices
item.description: The only petrol station in a small New South Wales town has run out of fuel amid ongoing oil supply disruptions caused by the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Smoke rising from Al Quoz area of Dubai after drone strikes
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Residents rescued and vehicles damaged after flash flooding in Bangalow
item.description: Residents are rescued from homes and vehicles after flash flooding in the Northern Rivers region in New South Wales.

item.title: Flash flooding damages cars and buildings in the NSW Northern Rivers
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Stood down senior project manager-turned-whistleblower condemns mould-ridden hospital
item.description: Former staff blow the whistle on horror conditions at a NSW hospital, including mouldy vents, inadequate separation of sewage and drinking water, and the use of sanitary pads to soak up rainwater leaking into wards.

item.title: Hawks brush off turblent week with demolition job on Bombers
item.description: Hawthorn puts a difficult week on and off the field behind it as it runs out comprehensive 62-point winners over Essendon at the MCG.

item.title: March flies swarm Pemberton local
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Friends pay tribute to Chinese backpackers killed in Kilkivan floodwater
item.description: Two Chinese holiday-makers killed in Queensland floodwater were in contact with their friends hours before their tragic deaths.

item.title: Southern Riverina 'left behind and forgotten', inquiry into Murrumbidgee health district split hears
item.description: An inquiry into the potential split of a southern New South Wales health district has heard that hospitals have been unable to hire staff to fill critical gaps.

item.title: Matildas survive nervy finish to book spot in Asian Cup semifinals
item.description: Goals from Sam Kerr and Alanna Kennedy allow the Matildas to scrap to a 2-1 win over North Korea in Perth in a thrilling Women's Asian Cup quarterfinal clash.

item.title: Friday Finance with David Chau
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Mackenzie Arnold's heroics, Sam Kerr and Alanna Kennedy's finishing saves Australia— Matildas vs North Korea quick hits
item.description: Mackenzie Arnold turns into a brick wall as Sam Kerr and Alanna Kennedy's ruthlessness saves Australia. Here are the quick hits from the Matildas' quarterfinal clash with North Korea.

item.title: Cuban president says talks were recently held with the US to resolve differences
item.description: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel says that his government has held recent talks with the US, marking the first time the Caribbean country confirms such speculation.

item.title: Hegseth says Iran's new supreme leader 'wounded and likely disfigured'
item.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth questions new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei's ability to lead after nearly two weeks of US and Israeli attacks on Iran, saying he is "wounded and likely disfigured".

item.title: Iran creates massive variable for looming federal budget
item.description: The war against Iran threatens to have a destabilising impact on this year's federal budget.

item.title: Iran, not the US, currently has the strategic upper hand
item.description: If there ever was a strategy behind Donald Trump's decision to blow up the Middle East, it is lying in tatters as the second week of the Iran war draws to a close.

item.title: Mercedes dominates F1 China sprint qualifying, Oscar Piastri qualifies fifth
item.description: Mercedes has once again dominated the Formula 1 field, qualifying their cars first and second for Saturday's sprint race in China.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: This week we're testing your knowledge of 70s rock, Pixar and our national anthem
item.description: Every question has only two options: one is right and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

item.title: Can exercise reduce period pain? And what kind is best?
item.description: Period pain can be crippling, but could exercise help relieve it? A reproductive health expert explains.

item.title: Who can best predict the 2026 Oscar winners — a mathematician or a critic?
item.description: Who is better at predicting this year's Oscar winners — a mathematician or a critic?

item.title: March one of the wettest on record as inland Australia faces second round of flooding
item.description: Another burst of tropical moisture and heavy rain will descend through the heart of Australia during the coming days.

item.title: Iranians fear war with Israel and US may end with Islamic regime still in power
item.description: When US and Israeli air strikes first tore through the skies over Iran, some regime opponents felt a rush of optimism. Two weeks later, that sense of hope is fading.

item.title: The moment Alex Johnston broke the NRL's try-scoring record was pure humanity
item.description: After all the speculation, the fans stormed the field in the aftermath of Alex Johnston's record-breaking try and the result was an outpouring of emotion celebrating a once-in-a-lifetime achievement.

item.title: Drought and floods create perfect storm for feral goats, pigs and camels to take over South Australia
item.description: Drought conditions in South Australia and flooding in the country's north have created a "perfect storm" for feral animal numbers to explode.

item.title: Ben Tudhope wins Paralympic Games bronze medal in snowboarding
item.description: Ben Tudhope has won his second medal of the Milano Cortina 2026 Paralympic Games, claiming bronze in the men's banked slalom SB-LL2.

item.title: Australian trade in jeopardy as Middle East 'war risk' costs surge
item.description: Australian exporters are facing increasing air and sea freight costs, as well as the possibility of diving consumer confidence.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Imperfect Matildas get perfect result in off-brand display to stay alive
item.description: Despite being largely outplayed by North Korea, the Australians have activated their belief mindset, and are zeroing in on the Women's Asian Cup trophy.

item.title: Smoke rises from US embassy in Iraq after attack
item.description: Two security officials say the embassy complex in Baghdad was struck in the attack, though the exact cause was not clear.

item.title: The latest injectable peptides trend on social media is worrying health experts
item.description: The latest wellness craze to take social media by storm is fuelling a growing grey market of injectable peptides in Australia, and it has experts worried.

item.title: Floodwater arrives at Longreach ahead of weekend peak
item.description: Longreach braces for flooding set to peak this weekend as residents in low-lying areas of the town are told to sandbag their homes.

item.title: Travis Fatchen looms large as Mount Gambier weighs up jailed MP's replacement
item.description: South Australia's second-largest city does not have an MP, but some say it has an incumbent.

item.title: An email from the wrong Stephen King was sent to journalists. This is the human impact of the bungle.
item.description: Bronwen Paterson spoke out about the devastation of her partner experiencing ramping in his final hours. Then, a government email bungle left her traumatised.

item.title: China unveils its plan to dominate the future of technology and AI
item.description: China unveils its five-year plan for tech dominance — including flying cars, brain-chip implants and humanoid robots.

item.title: First known image of Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, Peter Mandelson and Jeffrey Epstein released
item.description: An image appearing to show Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor and the sacked UK ambassador to Washington, Peter Mandelson, with convicted paedophile Jeffrey Epstein has been uncovered in the latest document dump released by the US DOJ.

item.title: Everyday items that may be affected by the fuel price due to war in the Middle East
item.description: Everyday items Australians can expect to be affected as the world faces a worsening fuel shortage due to war in the Middle East.

item.title: Dennis Richardson says sense of urgency 'didn't quite align' with royal commission
item.description: Dennis Richardson calls for a second interim report from the royal commission into antisemitism, warning it would be "unacceptable" to leave findings about any intelligence failures that contributed to the Bondi terrorist attack until the end of the year.

item.title: Melbourne would never have fallen for Kyle and Jackie O
item.description: You could argue Melbourne didn't warm to the the duo's shock-jock style but I think the answer is much simpler.

item.title: What does Katy Perry's loss in the High Court mean for her business?
item.description: Australian fashion designer Katie Perry has won her long-running trademark battle against pop star Katy Perry, but questions about whether the singer will be able to sell her clothing merchandise as part of any future tours remain.

item.title: Tasmanian wetland rebounds as invasive rice grass nears eradication
item.description: An invasive weed planted decades ago quickly spread across Tasmania's north-west, choking mudflats and wildlife habitat. Now, those involved in a long-running effort to wipe it out are seeing real results.

item.title: NT floods captured in photos and video from Katherine, Beswick, Palumpa, Daly River, Darwin River
item.description: Many regions across the Northern Territory were submerged by flooding this week. Here is some of the most dramatic footage from the extreme weather event, right across the Top End.

item.title: Confusion and anger as Bluesfest ticketholders question timing of cancellation
item.description: Festival revellers, who have spent thousands on the event, are angry and have been left wondering when organisers knew it was not viable.

item.title: How a tropical city found itself on water restrictions at the height of the wet season
item.description: Darwin's main water supply failed this week, forcing thousands of households onto unexpected water restrictions. Now questions are being asked about the response.

item.title: Wayne Bennett calls pitch invasion after Alex Johnston's NRL try-scoring record 'pretty disruptive'
item.description: A grumpy Wayne Bennett has told reporters he has already answered enough questions about Alex Johnston's try-scoring record, and described fans rushing the field as "pretty disruptive".

item.title: NT parliament increases minimum sentence for domestic violence murder, speaker berates backbencher
item.description: Northern Territory parliament has passed legislation increasing the mandatory minimum prison sentence for domestic violence murder from 20 to 25 years. During sittings this week, the speaker also berated a government backbencher over his handling of bullying claims.

item.title: Health authorities investigating source of mysterious eye infections in Darwin football players
item.description: Aussie Rules players in Darwin with symptoms of conjunctivitis are being urged to contact health authorities amid an outbreak of unknown origin affecting dozens of players.

item.title: Adrian Segečić switches from Australia to Croatia ahead of FIFA World Cup
item.description: One of Australia's brightest young talents appears to have been lost without even playing a game for the Socceroos, with Portsmouth dynamo Adrian Segečić switching associations to Croatia.

item.title: Katherine schools to reopen Monday but Daly River to stay above record level
item.description: Government schools in Katherine will reopen from Monday, while Katherine High School will operate under a "hybrid model", with the evacuation centre continuing on site.

item.title: Outback band launches debut EP inspired by language and country
item.description: A group of young musicians from Queensland's Gulf country are turning stories and language into song, and are now set mentor other aspiring performers.

item.title: Why does denim attract hordes of biting march flies?
item.description: Denim-wearing Louise Stokes was swarmed by the biting insects during a bushwalk near Pemberton, and it turns out clothing choice can play a significant role in deterring them.

item.title: Cultural burning practices return to Yuwibara Country for first time since colonisation
item.description: A practice that has helped care for land for more than 60,000 years returns to Yuwibara Country in north Queensland.

item.title: WA politicians chided for 'appalling' behaviour in Question Time. Don't we deserve better?
item.description: Question Time in WA is often loud, occasionally funny, and rarely productive. If this is how the people elected to look out for the best interests of West Australians behave at work, don't we deserve better?

item.title: Rotating home owners boast of 360-degree views and energy benefits
item.description: They may be costly to build, but rotating homes are loved for the way they can move with the sun, capture breezes from any direction and make the most of the scenery around them.

item.title: Biofuel industry awaits Commonwealth support as fuel tensions increase in Middle East
item.description: Australia once produced 80 per cent of its own fuel. Now it imports the same amount, and an unlikely answer may be sitting in your local takeaway shop.

item.title: Injuries mar Swans' thumping win over premiers
item.description: Sydney cruise to a second big home win and condemn reigning AFL premiers Brisbane to a second loss, but pay a price with injuries.while Adelaide records an impressive victory over Collingwood at the MCG.

item.title: Biscuit, tiramisu and pistachio stuffing: dates just got even better - ABC News
item.description: Forget chocolate bars, these stuffed dates are an easy treat and also make a beautiful gift.

item.title: Buffel grass fight reaches Canberra as cattle industry pushes back
item.description: Traditional owners and scientists want buffel grass recognised as a Weed of National Significance, but the cattle industry opposes the move because it relies on the species for fodder.

item.title: Majority of the Gulf's migrants are South Asian — their families fear for their safety and free speech
item.description: For many South Asian migrants across the Gulf, keeping quiet about Iran's attacks ensures they're able to pay the bills and feed their families. But is leaving even an option?

item.title: Haifa residents describes bomb hitting suburb
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Scene of destruction after missile hits Arab Israeli town of Haifa
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Trump posts video of strikes on Kharg Island
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: How Middle East airport closures are affecting foreign travellers
item.description: John McGuigan was due to go back to work weeks ago in Manchester. He's still stuck in Australia using up all his work leave entitlements due to the war in the Middle East.

item.title: New fuel standards will see higher levels of sulphur in Australia's petrol. What does that mean for your car?
item.description: The federal government's temporary easing of fuel standards will see 200 million litres of "dirty" fuel injected into the domestic market. Should drivers be worried?

item.title: 'Blowing my mind': Panthers thump Sharks in brutal Bathurst contest
item.description: The clash between Penrith and Cronulla blows the mind of former coach Matthew Elliott as the Panthers dominate a physical affair in Bathurst.

item.title: At 102 years old, Dean Simes might be Australia's oldest tech-help guy
item.description: Dean Simes bought his first personal computer this century. Now, the centenarian wants to help other seniors understand the technology "you can't avoid".

item.title: Kharg Island is a 'lifeline' for Iran's economy: Amin Saikal
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: What is Iran's Kharg Island used for and why has the US attacked it?
item.description: Donald Trump says the US has "obliterated" Iran's military assets on the island, but stopped short of attacking other infrastructure.

item.title: Kimi Antonelli on pole for F1 Chinese Grand Prix, Oscar Piastri qualifies fifth
item.description: Italian teenager Kimi Antonelli has become the youngest driver to secure pole position for a Formula 1 grand prix, topping the timesheets in qualifying for Sunday's Chinese Grand Prix.

item.title: NT Chief Minister Lia Finocchiaro requests ADF help with flood recovery as central Australia braces for more rain
item.description: Chief Minister Lia Finocchiaro is asking the federal government to deploy the ADF as floodwaters start to recede in parts of the Top End, while emergency services brace for more rain in central Australia.

item.title: One Nation's Cory Bernardi talks 'rookie error', Liberal MP dissents on candidate controversy ahead of South Australian election
item.description: A week out from election day in SA, One Nation's Cory Bernardi admits to a "rookie error", as a federal Liberal MP expresses reservations about the decision to part ways with a controversial candidate.

item.title: Indonesian human rights campaigner Andrie Yunus attacked with acid on Jakarta street
item.description: A prominent human rights activist has been attacked with acid in Jakarta, leaving him with serious burns.

item.title: Helicopter crashes near Drake, in north-east New South Wales
item.description: Emergency services will resume their search for a missing pilot after a crashed helicopter was found in north-east New South Wales.

item.title: Floodwaters reach roof height at Nauiyu/Daly River
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Matildas learn semifinal opponent as Chinese Taipei fans grapple with team name
item.description: The Matildas will face China PR in the semifinals of the Women's Asian Cup after the Steel Roses required extra time to see off a disciplined and spirited Chinese Taipei at Perth Rectangular Stadium.

item.title: Queensland Reds defeat NSW Waratahs 26-17 in Super Rugby Pacific round 5 clash at Lang Park
item.description: The Queensland Reds have continued their winning run in Super Rugby, defeating arch-rival NSW Waratahs in a contest that lacked quality for an hour, then produced exhilarating play for the final 20 minutes.

item.title: Trump threatens to hit Iran's Kharg Island oil network if shipping lanes remain blocked
item.description: US President Donald Trump threatens to strike the oil infrastructure of Iran's Kharg Island hub unless Tehran stopped attacking vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Political risks of war against Iran go beyond the petrol pump
item.description: If the instability in the Middle East keeps energy markets volatile, the economic and political consequences will extend far beyond the petrol pump.

item.title: Hard Quiz: Time to show us what you've got. Let's play, HARD!
item.description: One of these days, you might get lucky and ace this quiz. It's unlikely to be today. Let's play, HARD!

item.title: Back Roads: In Eldorado, Myf Warhurst meets the king of compost toilets
item.description: When Myf Warhurst arrived in the sleepy town of Eldorado to film the latest episode of Back Roads, she didn't anticipate she would be touching compost made from human waste. But Hamish Skermer has that effect.

item.title: Meet Margaret Thanos, director of A Mirror and The River and a rising star of Australia theatre
item.description: With two main-stage productions already under her belt in 2026, Margaret Thanos has cemented her place as a rising star of Australian theatre.

item.title: Iranian State TV reads out a statement from Iran's new leader
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Statement from Iran's new leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, fuels rumours about his whereabouts
item.description: Late last week, Iran's new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, broke his silence after days of intensifying rumours about his health and whereabouts, but it did little to put them to rest.

item.title: AI is helping choose targets in Iran war — now it's a target too
item.description: Iran struck three data centres in the Middle East, and has signalled that technology infrastructure will continue to be in the firing line.

item.title: 'I don't love it': Vikkstar on social media algorithms and the future of YouTube
item.description: One of the world's biggest YouTubers believes algorithms are creating an addictive cycle online.

item.title: The new health-boosting wheat ready to change the Western diet
item.description: Australian scientists have developed a wheat variety that dramatically boosts fibre in white bread and other flour-based foods.

item.title: Is Australia about to say goodbye to its experiment with low unemployment?
item.description: If war in the Middle East sends global inflation surging, will Australia have to say goodbye to its experiment with low unemployment?

item.title: Arnhem Land called Yothu Yindi co-founder Witiyana Marika home as band was going global
item.description: Rockstar. Leader. Peacemaker. Witiyana Marika was riding the wave of international fame in the band Yothu Yindi when his dying father called him back to Arnhem Land.

item.title: How the Iranian-Australian community united to help Iran's women footballers
item.description: The remaining members of Iran's women's football team might have left Australia, but the journey is far from over, as members of the Iranian-Australian community share what really went into the fight to protect their countrywomen.

item.title: Are we outsourcing our souls to artificial intelligence?
item.description: More people are turning to AI companions during personal crises, treating it as a confidante and even a way to speak to the dead. And while it helps some, it can also have devastating consequences, writes Compass presenter Benjamin Law.

item.title: Three members of Iranian women's football team return home after seeking asylum
item.description: Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke confirms that a trio of members of the Iranian women's football team, who were given humanitarian visas, chose to return home.

item.title: Israel launches fresh strikes on Iran after Trump says he's not ready to make a deal
item.description: US President Donald Trump said he was not ready for a deal with Tehran to end the war which has shut off the vital Strait of Hormuz and caused chaos in global energy markets.

item.title: Election campaigning in an ankle bracelet, Nick McBride wants a third term in MacKillop
item.description: ABC News joined Nick McBride on the campaign trail in Lucindale, a small sheep and cattle farming town 345 kilometres south-east of Adelaide, where locals gathered at the footy oval for the annual show.

item.title: Farmers hit with rising costs and lost production as battle against rabbit crisis worsens
item.description: Farmers say they are killing thousands of wild rabbits on their properties, but the problem is getting worse and they are seeking help.

item.title: From petrol prices to mining, here's how the Iran war is affecting WA
item.description: The war in the Middle East is beginning to have a tangible impact on the lives of Western Australians. Here's how.

item.title: Loved ones of Australian woman question backpacker Tobias Pick's manslaughter sentence
item.description: In 2020, a German backpacker strangled a Tasmanian sex worker, then fled with her mobile phone and cash. His version of events was accepted by an Australian court, which jailed him for manslaughter. Now, German authorities are re-examining the case.

item.title: Fly larvae turns Riverland food and farm waste into animal feed
item.description: A regional South Australian couple's fly larvae farm is using discarded produce to make a valuable food source.

item.title: F1 Chinese Grand Prix: Oscar Piastri hopeful of McLaren improvement, race start time in Australia
item.description: Oscar Piastri has said McLaren's starting positions on the third row for today's Formula 1 Chinese Grand Prix are "about where we kind of belong".

item.title: UAE minister Reem Al Hashimy describes Iran's attacks as 'almost unhinged', tells ABC they surprised her
item.description: Iran's actions in attacking neighbouring countries were unprecedented, "almost unhinged" and irrational, according to a senior United Arab Emirates minister.

item.title: Floodwaters spread into Longreach's outskirts with peak expected on Sunday
item.description: The swollen Thomson River has carried floodwaters closer to homes with levels forecast to continue rising.

item.title: First Australian woman finishes Oceans Seven swimming challenge despite shark attack
item.description: Joanne Norman cinched the international accolade by swimming across seven of the world's most dangerous channels.

item.title: F1 cancels Bahrain and Saudi Arabia grands prix due to Middle East war
item.description: Formula 1 has cancelled its grand prix weekends in Bahrain and Saudi Arabia, as war in the Middle East continues.

item.title: Inside Lainey Wilson's non‑stop rise through tours, Netflix doco, Stagecoach and new single
item.description: Arena tours, feature films and a new single — here's how one of America's biggest stars keeps climbing.

item.title: Eight players on Tony Popovic's radar to make the Socceroos 26-man 2026 World Cup squad
item.description: Tony Popovic wants his players to show "toughness" as all Socceroos sides have done in the past, but he wants them to shed the underdog tag. These players could help Australia do that at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: New discovery of rare Lord Howe Island stick insect colonies
item.description: Once so common they were used as bait, Lord Howe Island stick insects are making a comeback from the brink of extinction.

item.title: Australia's Georgia Gunew ends debut Winter Paralympics with 11th in women's slalom for vision-impaired skiers
item.description: Eager to follow in the footsteps of Paralympian great Melissa Perrine, rising Australian skier Georgia Gunew has shown her potential at the Milano-Cortina Games.

item.title: Cracks appear in Runit Dome amid sea level rise in Marshall Islands
item.description: Cracks have appeared in a concrete dome holding 120,000 tonnes of contaminated soil and debris from Cold War-era nuclear bomb tests.

item.title: If you feel like the world is spinning too quickly, look at who's setting the pace
item.description: Around the world, things are moving at a dizzying speed, and the pace is being set from Washington. Some people claim this is a deliberate strategy to overwhelm us, and that it comes with consequences.

item.title: Barnaby Joyce regrets not doing more to shore up fuel stockpile, amid inflation fears
item.description: One Nation MP Barnaby Joyce says he regrets not doing more when he was in government to secure fuel reserves, as Australia grapples with supply shortages triggered by war in the Middle East.

item.title: Iranian footballers wait in Malaysia for return home after Asian Cup
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Danish Queen Mary told the ABC her first trip to Uluru was "magical" as she and King Frederik begin the second day of their royal tour of Australia.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Perth family locked in battle with NDIS over care for disabled son
item.description: The parents of a disabled teenager have lifted the lid on the toll lobbying the NDIS for adequate support has taken on their lives, saying they feel exhausted and let down.

item.title: Plea from Tasmania's police commissioner for limit on gun ownership knocked back by government
item.description: In the push for gun law reform in the wake of the Bondi attack, Tasmania's police commissioner urged the state government to limit the number of firearms a person can own, citing the murder of an officer the year before in her plea — but to no avail, documents show.

item.title: Sweden's Ludvig Åberg leads The Players Championship by three shots after penultimate round
item.description: Ludvig Åberg has not won one of golf's major titles yet but the Swedish star is in prime position to take out the "fifth major", The Players Championship in Florida.

item.title: New Victorian laws could see businesses shut down if they're caught selling illicit tobacco
item.description: The Victorian government will introduce new laws giving the tobacco licensing regulator the power to shut down businesses caught selling illegal tobacco.

item.title: Motörhead guitarist Phil Campbell dies aged 64
item.description: In a statement on Instagram, his family says he "passed away peacefully following a long and courageous battle in intensive care after a complex major operation".

item.title: Petracca dominates as Suns cruise against Eagles, Demons win thriller against Saints
item.description: New recruit Christian Petracca is sensational again as Gold Coast smashes West Coast, after Melbourne completes a thrilling and memorable MCG win over St Kilda.

item.title: What is breastfeeding aversion and how can you manage it?
item.description: Anger, dread, anxiety, guilt, worry and disconnect are just some of the feelings described by women who experience breastfeeding aversion response.

item.title: Explosion targets Iranian space facility in Tehran
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: France wins back-to-back Six Nations Championships, clinching the title with a 48-46 win over England
item.description: After five rounds of matches, the Six Nations Championship comes down to a single penalty in the 83rd minute of the final game — and France emerges as back-to-back champions.

item.title: Former NRL player Chris Walker charged with alleged domestic violence offences
item.description: Former Queensland State of Origin player Chris Walker is due to face a Gold Coast court tomorrow after his arrest last night.

item.title: Fire at UAE oil hub
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Oil crisis makes rate hike a near certainty, but then what? - 15 March 2026
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'Half their salary cap gone': Knights count cost of win over Manly
item.description: Kalyn Ponga and Dylan Brown are both forced from the field in the first half as the Newcastle Knights ran riot against the Sea Eagles at Brookvale Oval, before the Dolphins storm back from 14-0 down to beat the Titans and become the first Queensland team to win a game this season.

item.title: Piastri fails to start again as teenager Antonelli takes maiden win
item.description: Oscar Piastri misses his second start of a Formula 1 grand prix in 2026, as McLaren suffers a double-blow and Mercedes celebrates another victory.

item.title: North-west town the first in Victoria to run out of fuel
item.description: Robinvale, located about 70 kilometres east from Mildura, ran out of fuel on Saturday evening.

item.title: Aftermath of Israeli attack on medical centre in Lebanon
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Body of pilot located after helicopter crash near Drake in north-east NSW
item.description: NSW Police say the body located after a helicopter crash in north-east NSW is yet to be identified, but they believe it's the 77-year-old pilot.

item.title: Hannah Green wins Women's Australia Open by one shot in Adelaide
item.description: Hannah Green says her nerves were frayed during her one-stroke victory at the Women's Australian Open in Adelaide.

item.title: Raissa Butkowski to contest Farrer for Liberals after Sussan Ley's resignation
item.description: Raissa Butkowski has been selected as the Liberal candidate for the seat of Farrer.

item.title: Three fishermen missing off Beachport along SA's Limestone Coast
item.description: Emergency crews are responding after three fishermen were declared missing at Beachport in South Australia's south-east.

item.title: Danish King Frederik and Queen Mary visit governor-general's house in Canberra as royal tour continues
item.description: Danish Queen Mary speaks of the similarities between the two countries as she and her husband King Frederik arrive in Canberra on day two of their Australian tour.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Japan dominates Philippines to cruise into semifinals and delight diaspora
item.description: Sayoko had never played football, but the Japan women's side inspired her to take up the game. And the Nadeshiko lived up to the hype at Stadium Australia, defeating the Philippines 7-0 on an afternoon of colour and national pride.

item.title: New bin units rolled out in Melbourne to promote bottle and can recyling
item.description: New "vertical" units have been placed on bins across Melbourne, allowing collectors to more easily and safely retrieve bottles and cans for Victoria's Container Deposit Scheme.

item.title: This week in history quiz: COVID cruise ships, Neighbours launches and walking in space
item.description: This week in years past saw the first ever spacewalk, the Harbour Bridge opening and more importantly the premiere of Neighbours. Test your memory.

item.title: These musicians stole our hearts on the first season of The Piano, so what are they up to now?
item.description: In the first season of The Piano, nobody quite knew what to expect. But what happens after you get the chance to play for a national audience? We catch up with some of the pianists to find out how music continues to shape their lives.

item.title: What Donald Trump's mixed messages on the war in Iran reveal about his aims
item.description: Two weeks after the US and Israel first struck Iran, Donald Trump's timeline and end goal for the war remain uncertain.

item.title: What the government knew about SA’s toxic algal bloom and what it told the public
item.description: The government knew a harmful toxin had been found in its algal bloom, but took months to update its health advice.

item.title: Inside the experiences of Roblox for parents and children
item.description: The ABC has spoken to more than half a dozen parents about their children's experience on Roblox following the Commonwealth's calls for a meeting with the platform amid reports of grooming and exposure to explicit content.

item.title: Iran war sees oil price surge and makes interest rate hike likely
item.description: A war in Iran has led to a cost-of-living spike in Australia and as petrol prices rise, interest rates are likely to be next.

item.title: Doctors want better protection for workers exposed to workplace chemicals labelled 'silent killers'
item.description: Under the proposed changes, there would be a five-fold reduction in the exposure limit for benzene, chlorine exposure would be reduced ten-fold, and silica exposure would be halved.

item.title: Australia's refugee policy is tested as Iran's humanitarian crisis expands
item.description: Does the same principle that led Australia to support Israel and the US in Iran also extend to offering more humanitarian assistance?

item.title: What is lifestyle creep? Here's why it happens and how to avoid it
item.description: If you tend to splurge on more indulgences any time you receive a pay rise, you may be suffering from lifestyle creep — and it could have a longstanding effect on your finances.

item.title: Vape ignites on Virgin flight, sparking emergency at Melbourne Airport
item.description: A Virgin Australia flight makes an emergency landing at Melbourne Airport after a vape caught fire onboard.

item.title: One Nation MP Barnaby Joyce predicts party will 'poll number one' in many seats at SA election
item.description: Federal MP Barnaby Joyce is predicting a big primary vote for One Nation at this Saturday's South Australian election, but lead candidate Cory Bernardi would not be drawn on how many lower house seats he hoped the party would win.

item.title: South Sydney High School named on the HSC most improved school list for second year in a row
item.description: The Department of Education has released the HSC most improved list, which detailed the schools showing the most growth in the numbers of students receiving the top three bands in the HSC.

item.title: What you need to know about Iran today, with Matt Doran
item.description: The ABC's Middle East correspondent Matt Doran is in Amman and is providing daily updates on the Middle East war.

item.title: Study of Brisbane family helps researchers uncover genetic mutation linked to ulcerative colitis
item.description: Researchers have used advanced genetic sequencing on a Brisbane family to identify a gene mutation linked to ulcerative colitis, a type of inflammatory bowel condition, sparking hopes of new treatments.

item.title: Live: Iran says it 'never asked' for ceasefire as Trump says US refused deal
item.description: The Iranian foreign minister says he sees "no reason why we should talk with Americans".

item.title: F1 Chinese Grand Prix quick hits: More drama for Oscar Piastri and McLaren, as history is made in China
item.description: Oscar Piastri's wretched start to the 2026 Formula 1 season continues in China, with McLaren failing to complete a lap of the grand prix.

item.title: Woman rescued from floodwaters at Mossman in Far North Queensland
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: BOM predicts potential cyclone may form on Tuesday but path uncertain
item.description: Flooded communities across Queensland are watching rivers begin to recede after days of heavy rain, but the Bureau of Meteorology has predicted a cyclone may form later in the week.

item.title: Fuel rationing a chance in Australia if war continues to trim global oil supplies, experts say
item.description: Experts say Australians should prepare for the possibility of widespread fuel rationing if the war in the Middle East is prolonged.

item.title: Nepali security guard killed in Abu Dhabi shows risks faced by 'trapped' migrant workers
item.description: The killing of a Nepali security guard in Abu Dhabi highlights the growing risks faced by millions of migrant workers across the Gulf as the conflict between Iran, Israel and the United States escalates.

item.title: Fifth member of Iranian women's soccer team leaves Australia
item.description: A fifth member of the Iranian women's football team withdrew her asylum claim and left the country overnight, the federal government says.

item.title: Popular mango farm forced to sell as compliance measures pressure producers
item.description: Mango industry representatives say addressing mounting compliance requirements is like "trying to turn back the Titanic".

item.title: Live: ASX set to slide as war rattles global stocks, RBA rate hike looms
item.description: Global investors continue to rotate out of shares as the Iranian conflict continues and rising oil prices stoke inflation fears. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: Three New South Wales fishermen found dead off SA's Beachport
item.description: SA Police say the alarm was raised on Sunday afternoon, when the three men did not return to shore at Beachport in the state's south-east. A local fisher said he tried to warn the men before they left that conditions were rough.

item.title: Live: Australia will not send navy ship to Strait of Hormuz, minister says
item.description: Australia will not be sending a navy ship to the Strait of Hormuz to protect oil-carrying vessels from Iranian attacks, the transport minister says. Follow live.

item.title: UEFA says intercontinental football champions match between Argentina and Spain is off due to Middle East war
item.description: A meeting of men's football's South American and European champions — the Finalissima — featuring superstars Lionel Messi and Lamine Yamal, has been called off due to security concerns amid the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Impassioned plea from Tasmania's top cop no match for political realities on gun ownership caps
item.description: Gun law reform is always fraught, especially in Tasmania. An impassioned plea from the state's top cop hasn't been enough to sway the government on gun ownership caps, but opposition and crossbench MPs may be able to pass the limits into law.

item.title: Gippsland woman completes mother's unfinished blankets for those in need
item.description: When craft enthusiast Maxine Narelle Smith died, she left behind a collection of knitted squares. Her daughter and a team of volunteers are continuing her work, knitting them into blankets for those in need.

item.title: Live: Oscars underway as Amy Madigan and KPop Demon Hunters win early
item.description: Australians Rose Byrne and Jacob Elordi will be hoping to cap off their awards season success with the ultimate prizes as the 98th Academy Awards get underway. Follow live.

item.title: Queensland arts minister approved name for Glasshouse Theatre before public vote opened, documents show
item.description: Documents released under right to information show Queensland's arts minister was "set" on the name in January, months before the community was invited to have their say.

item.title: Homeless Indigenous elder died after years on 'priority' WA public housing waitlist
item.description: The Wongatha man from WA's Goldfields died in Kalgoorlie-Boulder, about 600km east of Perth, on February 6 after battling emphysema and lung cancer.

item.title: Comebacks, medals and hopes for the future — how Australia fared at the Milano-Cortina Winter Paralympics
item.description: From Ben Tudhope's two snowboard medals to Michael Milton's 20-year comeback, here's a look at how Australia went at the 2026 Milano-Cortina Winter Paralympics.

item.title: Oscars 2026 red carpet: Rose Byrne, Nicole Kidman represent Australia on Academy Awards red carpet
item.description: Before the ceremony of the 98th Oscars is the spectacle that is the red carpet. See all the most striking looks here.

item.title: Kimberley farmers face three-year wait for new weather radar
item.description: Farmers in WA's north typically rely on real-time radar data for planting purposes during the wet season.

item.title: Fish price spike, supply issues months after WA's demersal fishing ban
item.description: The ABC has spoken to six fishmongers who have had to raise prices after the WA government's demersal fishing ban, with consumers set to pay 10 to 40 per cent more for most species.

item.title: Oscars 2026 winners list: Amy Madigan wins Best Supporting Actress, KPop Demon Hunters honoured
item.description: For the updated winners list as the ceremony takes place, click here.

item.title: Cameron Young wins Players Championship in Florida as leader Ludvig Åberg hits in the water twice
item.description: Sweden's Ludvig Åberg loses a three-shot lead after hitting in the water on consecutive holes at The Players Championship, as American Cameron Young takes out the "fifth major".

item.title: Family's quest for sensory-safe playgrounds following daughter's tragic death at The Grotto lookout
item.description: The family of a six-year-old girl with autism who fell from the cliffs at a popular NSW South Coast lookout a year ago want to see more inclusive public spaces.
